notes: These are process-owned CFB27 remotes observed during main menu to loaded Dynasty traces, not confirmed protocol endpoints.
The observed_blocked_dynasty_loading_* entries appeared during a controlled block test that left Dynasty on a permanent loading screen; treat them as fallback or follow-up candidates until repeated.
The repeated_dynasty_rotated_* entries appeared when the older candidate set was blocked and CFB27 still loaded Dynasty, indicating endpoint rotation/failover.
The repeated_trial_* entries came from Trial-aware traces against CollegeFB27_Trial.exe and show another rotation cluster/failover set.

The repeated_trial_press_start_presence_* entries came from a normal EA-launched CollegeFB27_Trial.exe Press Start trace and are likely online presence/login service dependencies, not proof of Dynasty hosting traffic.

The repeated_trial_press_start_to_dynasty_* entries came from a normal EA-launched CollegeFB27_Trial.exe Press Start through Dynasty trace and are the strongest current candidates for the complete online flow.
The repeated_trial_diagnostics_online_flow_* entries came from diagnostics-assisted traces with local Cypress services running while the game itself was launched normally.

Known telemetry/CDN/identity/TOS entries such as 100.56.194.93:443, 23.61.2.191:443, 23.220.162.132:443, 23.192.146.155:443, 184.29.30.190:443, and 54.227.239.228:443 are intentionally excluded from the block-candidate list unless later tests prove they gate Online Dynasty.
Use Block Candidates only for controlled tests, and run CypressLauncher.exe as administrator so Windows Firewall rules can be created.
Click Unblock immediately after each controlled block test.
Local entries such as 127.0.0.1:3216, 127.0.0.1:3217, UDP *:*, and 192.168.1.205:80 SYN_SENT are intentionally excluded.
